steps.title: Identify the cautionary signal
steps.content: The key signal is that researchers “cautioned against applying the findings to customers generally” despite studying only Serbian participants from a developing economy. This caution reveals that the finding's scope is limited to Serbia, not universal.

steps.title: Match the logical implication
steps.content: When researchers caution against generalizing, they implicitly suggest that further research is needed to validate broader applicability. Choice A directly addresses this by proposing research with participants from countries of varying economic development—the specific investigation required to determine whether the finding applies generally. The other choices either make unsupported predictions about what other countries' preferences actually are (B), propose business recommendations unrelated to research validity (C), or address sample-size questions within Serbia rather than geographic generalization (D). That matches choice A — A is correct.
